name: Kenny Davern
abstract: John Kenneth Davern (January 7, 1935 – December 12, 2006) was an American jazz clarinetist.

quotes.text: I think you said it, it’s at a standstill. It’s perhaps in a state perhaps of decomposition because the other night I heard, again, airport to home and scan the airwaves, I heard a concert from I think it was either Carnegie Hall or Lincoln Center with Wynton Marsalis and they were playing the music of Jelly Roll Morton, which is what I cut my eyeteeth on so to speak when I was growing up. And I mean I really love that music. I could tell you the alternate takes you know, that kind of thing, that’s how engrained and how much I loved Jelly Roll’s music as a kid and I still do. And I heard them, when I say “them” that whole group of guys who formed that organization who are like what would you call them, part of the repertory company, take that music and actually decimate it to the point where I was outraged that they should play this music so badly and, even if they read the notes they were being played without really the true passion that the music had, and the musicianship was noticeably inferior to the original. And if you can’t do it as good as the original, do it different than, but don’t try to do it the same way and have it fall short of the mark, you see. And I just said they’re making a travesty of the music that I grew up and loved, you know. The same as using alternate takes as — supposing you were a kid and you never heard this stuff. It always takes some recording that goes WHAM, that gives you the emotional hook so to speak, where you bite, and you say that’s it, this music is IT. And it comes at different times. Hopefully it comes at an earlier age, then you have much more time to develop and you know to work things out. But I’ve heard, even Louis Armstrong’s second and third takes, which were not the ones that the artist intended to be issued, and being the artist is now deceased and has no representation of his own music, people seem to feel free that they can take a second or third or what they call alternative/alternate take, and use it as the one that was set up as the standard. The one that grabbed me. The one that said when I hear this played, this song played, that’s the definitive version of it. It will never be better than that. And that’s what I love about © Fillius Jazz Archive -7-
quotes.caption: Critique of Wynton Marsalis performing Jelly Roll Morton
quotes.doc_title: Kenny Davern interviewed by Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, September 23, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Metz Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: To have been able to record a record with what essentially was Soprano Summit. Davern, Wilber, Haggart on bass, Dave Frishberg on piano and Bucky, and me on drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Metz Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, October 3, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Haggart
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve been over since then too with a lot of the same guys, Kenny Davern and Yank Lawson... lends his arranging skills to such projects as Kenny Davern’s album with strings, “My Inspiration.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Haggart interviewed by Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, September 22, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Wilber
about_quotes.evidence: His extensive list of recordings include collaborations with Kenny Davern... Yeah, that’s the Summit Reunion which is our later name for Soprano Summit, Kenny Davern and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Wilber interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 22, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Evan Christopher
about_quotes.evidence: And we had a blast. And I never, there was never a warming up period. He was kind to me pretty much from the beginning... We always enjoyed each other company.
about_quotes.doc_title: Evan Christopher interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 1, 2009

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Bennett
about_quotes.evidence: I got to meet Kenny Davern about a year before he passed. And I remember he was really big on — he was very friendly to me
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Bennett interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 16, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Frishberg
about_quotes.evidence: people like Kenny Davern for instance, who I’ve known for forty years, we talk about the same things all the time
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Frishberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001
